The Psycho Cybernetic (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 4
Speaker 1: Hey guys and welcome to themeplate 17. This is called The Psycho-Cybernetic.
Borrowing a little term from Maxwell Maltz on this one. What we're doing on this
themeplate is really honoring the resistance and difficulty your prospects is likely to
have around the investment decision.
So instead of downplaying how hard it is to make a decision, we simply validate it and
then call on our prospect to temporarily put on the mask, or assume, the identity of
someone capable of making that decision. So this is going to hit heavily on the buyer
identification and binary choice triggers that we spoke about earlier in this module.
You know, instead of really kind of shaming and resisting prospects who still have
resistance at this stage, we totally integrate it and invite them to transform to someone
who can overcome it. So we're literally coaching kind of a mini transformation on the
spot. We're transforming them into someone of capable of making that binary choice.
So this is how it is going to play out.
We will start off with that remind of that urgency, without pouring it on too thick, once
again. Future pace the outcome or big transformation, who they will become on the
other side of this journey. Link it to the person they must become right now to achieve
it. This is kind of the big tandem here. Sell against indecisiveness, you know. Call on
them to become decisive and offer that binary choice. So this is that big literal call to
action, you know. You must decide. This isn't something you want to beat around the
bush around; you want to say it as clearly as possible. Then you can close with the
minimum viable commitment that you've already worked through on several of these
themeplates.
An optional PS for a little bit of empathy is to show your own struggle with
indecisiveness and how you may have overcome it in the past. And closing to a second
call to action around the binary choice.
The optional PS is something that could work really from an empathy perspective. We
tend to put ... We tend to forget how much we struggle with blind decisions and we
expect our prospects to somehow not feel any fear or resistance around making a
choice. So this is just about us having empathy for our prospect. Them seeing that, oh
wow. He kinda struggles with that too, yet that hasn't, yet he or she, hasn't allowed that
to hinder them in the longterm. So, that's an element you may want to weave in. Here is
how we have put it into action.
So, two hours. Mick Jagger, Michael Jordan, and the Matrix. Or it's just a click away, it's
just a click away if you want to get cheeky about it.
Body, about ninety minutes left until we shut it down so let me be snappy. When you
enroll in 10X Launches you can expect that in about four to six weeks from now, or less,
you'll be the rare breed of online course creator, coach consultant, or entrepreneur with
the power to ...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

And here where future pacing those outcomes. Fill up those email icons on your funnel
map with the magical words that make your PayPal blink. Write those words faster and
with more confidence then it took you to hire it out last time, so contrasting with that
moment of highest tension that they're already familiar with from your previous
messaging. Never feel bottle necked or helpless again. Kicking yourself for trying to
outsource the single task most responsible for bringing revenue into your business and
putting 20 year single malts in your single whiskey glass. Cheers to that.
It's a helluva transformation if you ask me. So this is that big transformation that they're
gonna get once they enroll, but to be honest, that's the easy part. It will only be possible
if you first become the kind of entrepreneur brave enough to get over the anxiety hump
and lock down your spot in 10X Launches right now. The only kind of business owner
capable of making a decision.
No, sorry letting that ugly countdown timer hit zero, then shrugging your shoulders and
saying, "Oops guess I missed it" isn't the same thing. So we're very blatantly selling end
games and decisiveness here. Selling them against a very typical reality of that. Literally
97% of your prospects are going to experience. Selling against that and instead selling
them on the immediate transformation they need to make right now, which involves a
decision.
So here's the deal. I'm gonna spare you the motivational hyperbole. The red pill versus
the blue bs or the you miss 100% of the shots you never take Instagram quote card
material. We're not in the Matrix, or are we? And you're not Michael Jordan, or are
you? Instead I'm going to invite you for just a few seconds, for just a few seconds,
anyone could do anything for just a few seconds. To be the type of confident business
owner capable of making a decision. To either ...
And here's the binary choice. Really kind of lay it out in front of them. To the best we
can at scale using online marketing. Obviously we can't show up in front of them like a
coach would and say, "Are you in or are you out?" And wait for a decision. But we could
lay those options on the table and really ask them. Make that appeal for them to choose
one.
So jump into 10X Launches knowing that if you're not adding extra digits to your next
funnel or product launch, you can undo without any real consequence. Minimum viable
commitment factored in with some outcome. So just framing that decision, that call to
action, while also leasing in the benefit and the risk reversal.
Or to say, enough already! I enjoyed all the free stuff in those workshops. Please get out
of my life so I can continue second guessing every word I write and blaming other
people when my funnel never gets off the ground.
So yeah, we kinda exaggerated a little bit, but not even, what that alternative would
look like. You know, this is something that a lot of our customers used to experience.
Wondering why this isn't working. I mean, it could be a million factors in that funnel and

then you just kinda get overwhelmed in even trying to do a post mortem and you just
wait for the next shiny object to come along.
But the bottom line here is your contrasting the two choices. Holding them accountable
to make one. So, can you do that for me? Can you make a choice? And I get it. Nobody is
100% confident 100% of the time. This is empathy, once again. Not holding them to this
impossible standard that not even you are capable of. Not even freaking Neo or MJ, but
all you need is three minutes of being confident enough. So, that's kind of minimum
viable confidence, if you will, to finish up your enrollment, grab your log in info, and
then meet us over in the Facebook group so we can give you a proper hero's welcome.
So future pacing those immediate next steps. Making it feel safe. Saying you don't need
to be super man, you just need three minutes of being confident enough to finish it up.
Cool, we'll be waiting here when you're ready. And that's it.
Then, confession. You know empathize even more with that indecisiveness. Even when I
know somethings right for me in my business, I still wait until the last second before
pulling the trigger. Identification with the buyer. I'm not your typical action take. I mull, I
muse, and I mess up a perfectly good Google doc trying to assess and analyze the
decision from every possible angel.
And then, after all that inevitably fails, I brew a pot of extra bold Kicking Horse Coffee,
put on the Bose headphones, and blast some Gimme Shelter as I climatically hit auto fill
on the checkout page. 'Cause in the wise words of one Mick Jagger, "you're just a click
away, you're just a click away." Hard not to sing along with that.
So this is really showing empathy, showing that this is also something you experience
and this is how you get through it. You know, this may or may not be exactly what
they're going to do, but they can kind of relate with maybe this brand of coffee,
headphones, the song on Spotify, auto fill on checkout pages. You know, sometimes we
have a, you know, those Chrome auto fills so it's just very familiar and grounded.
PPS in about an hour, I'm going to send out a final warning. If I don't people will freak
out and complain I didn't warn them. And you can even show the screenshot from that
email that's about to go out. That really kind of gives them an inside transparent peek,
puts them on your side.
Here's the thing, I don't want you to get it. By that time, I want you to either be diving
headfirst into the membership area and sharing your personal power jam in the
Facebook group. I've consciously decided that nope, not for me, not today, not ever and
feeling damn proud about your ability to walk away from a raging inferno with middle
fingers in the air.
So, once again, closing with that binary choice. Telling them their is going to be another
warning, you don't want them to get it, you're holding them to that standard of deciding
right here and right now.

The Psycho Cybernetic (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 4 of 4
So this is the Psycho-Cybernetic. Asking them to just put on that mask for a few minutes
of someone confident and capable enough to decide. And having multiple call to actions
of just reinforcing the fact that a binary choice exists. Once again, binary choice is the
reason why one on one selling, with follow ups, can close 20 to 50% where as online
marketing closes at 2 to 5%. Anywhere between that.
It's because, in the first version it ends in a choice. It doesn't end in a whimper and a
pixilated timer counting down to zero and you guys never having that final conversation.
So this email is meant to emulate the essence of that. Obviously we can't do it fully at
scale because you're not going to show up in the living rooms of everybody on your
email list, but this is at least going to create that vibe. And initiate those triggers of
what's happening in that one on one scenario. So that we could do it at scale. Do it with
50,000 people in a simple afternoon. That is what we are doing with coaching the
conversions.
So I will leave you with that. We have two more themeplates left. I will give you a
chance to give this one a crack and when you're done, when you're feeling confidant
with that, meet me in themeplate 18 where we will reintroduce you to one of the most
famous TV dad's from the 90s. So, I will see you there.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
